callouts.content: SKILL: /engage — Session onboarding ritual. Re-run here because the session is transitioning from Lab 05a (design) to Lab 05b (build) without a restart.

SKILL: /nextwave — Executes the next pending wave of parallel spec-driven sub-agents. Each agent runs on an isolated git worktree to prevent conflicts. The parent agent reviews all output before merging.

The Flight model splits a wave into sequential flights when parallel agents would conflict on shared files. Issues #13 and #14 both touch index.ts, so they can't run simultaneously. Flight 1 establishes the pattern, Flight 2 builds on it. This is how you get parallelism without merge conflicts.

The agent committed and merged without presenting the checklist or getting approval — a direct violation of the mandatory pre-commit protocol. The user's correction is firm but understanding: 'I understand why you felt you had it. Lets stick to the protocol going forward.' Rules exist for the times when momentum makes you want to skip them.

The install script wrote hooks in the old format (flat object) instead of the new format (matcher + hooks array). Automated tests couldn't catch this — it's a runtime integration issue between two systems. This is exactly why manual verification procedures exist.

A real production emergency mid-lab: the classifier subprocess hangs and blocks the entire MCP server. Watch how the troubleshooting unfolds — this wasn't scripted, it's a genuine incident that happened during the lab recording.

The fix isn't a patch — it's an architectural refactor to a producer-consumer pattern. The classifier becomes an independent consumer that processes a queue, decoupled from the MCP request cycle. This eliminates the hang by removing the synchronous dependency.

Quiescence mode is designed to minimize recording overhead when the agent isn't actively troubleshooting. The /wtf imout command suspends the flight recorder — hooks still fire but entries are classified as 'noise' and filtered from the timeline.

artifacts.title: Wave Execution Plan
artifacts.description: The 5-wave implementation plan driving this lab
artifacts.content:
## Wave Plan (11 issues across 5 waves)

| Wave | Theme | Issues | Parallel? |
|------|-------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | Foundation | #11 (scaffold + DB + CI) | Solo |
| 2 | Core Tools | #12 (wtf_now), #13 (wtf_freshell), #14 (wtf_happened) | 3-way |
| 3 | Automation | #15 (hook+queue), #16 (classifier) | 2-way |
| 4 | Integration | #17 (runbook), #18 (skills), #19 (install), #20 (docs) | 4-way |
| 5 | Verification | #21 (manual testing) | Solo |

**Total: 81 tests across all waves**
